prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of phenol was computed using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional and basis (tier standard, density-fitted) in PySCF, in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Both the neutral molecule (N) and the N±1 cation/anion states converged without issue. Mulliken charge analysis identified the hydroxyl oxygen (atom 1) as the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.162, and the ortho-carbon atoms (atoms 3 and 4, positions C1 and C2 relative to O) as the most electrophilic sites with f⁺ = 2.103 and 1.620, respectively. The dual descriptor at the ortho-carbons (2.032 and 1.549) confirms their electron-deficient character in the N+1 state, making them favorable for nucleophilic aromatic attack. Charge conservation indices (sum of f⁺ and f⁻ both equal to 1.000) confirm SCF convergence and chemical consistency. The Fukui heatmap visualization is available at the output path listed.</prose>
